title: Study of RAS(ON) Inhibitors in Patients With Gastrointestinal Solid Tumors

power_analysis.assessment: N/A — Phase 1 trial, not powered for efficacy

adaptive_designs.description: Adaptive design detected: Types identified: Dose-Finding / Phase I.

safety_adverse_events.ae_ascertainment: Not explicitly described (assumed standard investigator reporting)
safety_adverse_events.safety_monitoring: Investigator-reported with sponsor oversight (no DSMB mentioned)

eligibility.justification: Age is a standard feasibility criterion for clinical trials.
This criterion ensures patients are not too ill to participate in the trial, protecting them from potential harm.

eligibility.text: Adequate organ function as outlined by the study
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures patients have sufficient organ function for treatment and data collection.

eligibility.text: Pathologically or cytologically documented pancreatic carcinoma or poorly differentiated pancreatic carcinoma with metastatic disease or RAS-mutated, histologically or cytologically confirmed colorectal adenocarcinoma with documented unresectable or metastatic disease (Subprotocol A, B, and C)
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures patients have a specific type of cancer that is the focus of the trial.

eligibility.text: Presence of RAS G12D mutation (Subprotocol D, E, F)
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures patients have a specific genetic mutation that is being studied for treatment efficacy.
This criterion excludes patients with CNS tumors, protecting them from potential harm and ensuring the study's focus on specific cancers.

eligibility.text: Impaired gastrointestinal (GI) function that may significantly alter the absorption of RMC drugs
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures patients have adequate GI function to absorb the medication, impacting feasibility.

eligibility.text: Major surgery within 28 days of first dose
eligibility.justification: This criterion protects patients from potential harm by ensuring they are not undergoing major surgery before starting treatment.
This is a general statement that could encompass various factors impacting feasibility, such as patient compliance or adherence to study protocols.
